description: Market-structure scores are directional 1-5 reads for deciding where to build: attractiveness and growth are better when higher; competition, resource intensity, and distribution difficulty are harder when higher. They are not investment advice or canonical TAM estimates.

markets.name: Agent Identity & Permissions
markets.summary: Strong demand, but IAM and security incumbents are absorbing the category quickly.
markets.profit_wedge: Avoid basic agent identity; look for cross-vendor signed action receipts, delegated-intent policy, and audit standards that plug into Okta, Entra, MCP, SIEM, and SaaS tools.

markets.name: Miner-to-AI Datacenter Conversion
markets.summary: Power-rich bitcoin miners are converting sites into GPU/HPC capacity; the value is in buyer-trustable megawatts plus operating credibility.
markets.profit_wedge: Technical diligence, retrofit engineering, SLA certification, and brokerage for power-rich sites can monetize before becoming a capital-intensive operator.

markets.name: Model Routing & Inference Brokers
markets.summary: Developer demand is obvious, but model labs and clouds can squeeze margins unless routing, evals, spend controls, or governance become sticky.
markets.profit_wedge: The durable wedge is not a thin API wrapper; it is policy-aware routing, audit, fallback, eval feedback, and enterprise spend governance.

markets.name: Clinical Workflow AI
markets.summary: Big workflow budgets and painful labor shortages, but EHR integration, compliance, procurement, and clinical risk make distribution the moat.
markets.profit_wedge: Specialize around high-ROI workflow bottlenecks with measurable time saved, coding uplift, or throughput gains rather than generic medical chat.

markets.name: AI Drug Discovery & Biology Models
markets.summary: Potentially enormous outcomes, but wet-lab loops, clinical timelines, proprietary data, and pharma partnerships make it hard to bootstrap.
markets.profit_wedge: Best for proprietary data or platform partnerships; services-only AI biology usually lacks the upside of owning programs or validated assets.

markets.name: Autonomous Mobility
markets.summary: Robotaxis, trucking, and AV stacks have giant TAM but brutal capital, safety, insurance, regulation, and fleet operations requirements.
markets.profit_wedge: Infrastructure tooling, validation, maps, simulation, fleet ops, or narrow commercial autonomy is more reachable than launching a full robotaxi network.

markets.name: Robot Foundation Models
markets.summary: Embodied models could become the control layer for many robots, but data collection, hardware integration, and safety loops are expensive.
markets.profit_wedge: Tooling, data collection, teleop, evals, and deployment infrastructure may monetize earlier than owning a general robot model.

markets.name: Drones & Aerospace Autonomy
markets.summary: Defense demand, autonomy, and fleet operations are pulling drones and aircraft autonomy forward, but certification and procurement remain hard.
markets.profit_wedge: Mission autonomy, simulation, autonomy safety cases, and dual-use fleet software are better wedges than commodity airframes.

markets.name: Autonomy Simulation & Validation Tooling
markets.summary: Every physical autonomy company needs simulation, validation, synthetic data, safety cases, and scenario management before deployment.
markets.profit_wedge: This is the most atlas-native physical-AI wedge: sell tools to many autonomy programs instead of betting on one robot, car, or aircraft.

markets.name: Vertical Agent Eval Standards
markets.summary: Coding has benchmark gravity; finance, support, healthcare, legal, and ops agents still lack trusted executable task suites.
markets.profit_wedge: Start with a vertical where buyers already pay for quality assurance, then turn eval traces into procurement standards.

markets.name: On-device ML / Edge AI Runtime
markets.summary: ODML is moving from classic mobile vision/audio models to local GenAI, but Apple, Google, Meta/PyTorch, Microsoft/ONNX, and Qualcomm own much of the runtime and hardware surface.
markets.profit_wedge: Best wedge is not another runtime; it is model optimization, device benchmarking, privacy-preserving evals, fleet deployment, or vertical SDKs for regulated/offline edge use cases.
